reasoning: In 2024, while the Ukraine transit pipeline was still functional, the EU imported around 25 bcm of Russian gas in the first half of the year, averaging 12.5 bcm per quarter. Since then, the Ukraine transit route ended in January 2025, cutting off roughly 12-14 bcm/year (or about 3-3.5 bcm/quarter) of supply. Russian gas is now solely delivered to the EU via TurkStream (capacity to the EU is roughly 12-15 bcm/year) and as LNG (which reached ~14 bcm in the first eight months of 2025). This combined volume amounts to roughly 8-9 bcm per quarter, nowhere near the required 15 bcm threshold. Given the EU's ongoing phaseout of Russian fossil fuels under the REPowerEU plan and the hard physical limitations on delivery routes without Ukraine transit, hitting 15 bcm in any single quarter of 2025 is mathematically and politically highly implausible. The community forecast of 1% is accurate, and I am submitting 5% as it represents the minimum allowed bound under the prompt's rules.
